newsSince: No material new company-specific news since June 9. The $35M SeaPower battery order (3 customers) was announced January 2026 and the $28M order in April; both predate the window. The $615M Covelya acquisition remains on track to close by end of Q2 2026 (previously disclosed). Next earnings Aug 20, 2026.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the test-time-scaling/robotics lens, no new fundamental catalyst since June 9; battery orders and Covelya deal predate the window. Autonomous subsea sensing/robotics fit is intact (35% YoY Q1 growth, $165-175M FY26 guide) but unchanged. Hold at 21.
verdicts.bull: 35% YoY Q1 revenue growth, $165-175M FY26 guide, subsea battery/SAS demand plus Covelya scale-up; defense UUV tailwind.
verdicts.neutral: Solid execution but physical-AI/scaling link is indirect; no new datapoint moves the thesis this cycle.
verdicts.bear: Modest +7% YTD, integration risk on $615M Covelya deal; lumpy defense order timing.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Covelya close (end-Q2) and continued SeaPower/UUV order momentum.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Acquisition integration and order lumpiness ahead of Aug 20 print.
